company.name: Lumen Collective
company.ceo_priority_q4: Execution velocity — every team must show measurable weekly progress against top 1-2 priorities

employees.name: Adam Ankeny
kpis: Onboard 25 new creators/week to the platform
employees.writing_style: Concise, numbers-forward. Leads with metrics, short sentences. Consistent format daily.
employees.hidden_truth: HIGH PERFORMER. Hits or exceeds targets every day. Control group — should rank #1.

employees.name: Avery Holmseth
kpis: Drive 15% expansion revenue from existing book
employees.writing_style: Long narrative paragraphs. Buries metrics in prose. Optimistic tone ('great call,' 'positive energy,' 'feeling good'). Avoids numbers and dates.
employees.hidden_truth: OPTIMISM-REALITY GAP. Updates sound great but metrics are slipping. Northwind Athletics renewal stalled (4 days 'almost there'). Petalcrest expressed concerns but Avery downplays it.

employees.name: Sean Cretti
employees.writing_style: Dry, table-like, numbers-forward. ROAS, CPM, CTR everywhere. No fluff.
employees.hidden_truth: SUBMISSION GAP. Skips Tuesday and Wednesday entirely — does not post any update on those two days. Posts on Monday, Thursday, and Friday only. Thursday and Friday updates cover what happened on the skipped days. Numbers are fine but cadence violation is the issue. You MUST only generate 3 updates (Monday, Thursday, Friday) — do NOT generate Tuesday or Wednesday updates.

employees.name: Jeff Collard
employees.writing_style: Short, breezy, casual. One-liners. 'Crushed it today, 45 dials 💪'. Sometimes forgets to mention meetings booked.
employees.hidden_truth: VANITY METRICS GAP. Hits dial count every day (activity metric) but meetings-booked is quietly declining: 5 → 4 → 3 → 2 → 2. Never draws attention to it.

employees.name: Hannah Kargman
kpis: Ship Creator Matching v2 by end of quarter
employees.writing_style: Thoughtful, qualitative. 'Alignment,' 'blockers,' 'stakeholder input,' 'iterating.' Rarely commits to dates. Talks process more than output.
employees.hidden_truth: NO FORWARD MOTION + MISSED KPI. Same blocker (aligning with eng on matching algorithm) mentioned Mon-Fri with no escalation or progress. User research KPI: only 2 completed sessions total (Mon, Tue). Wed-Fri: rescheduling, silence, no recovery to 5.

standup_sets.text: 7 creators onboarded today. Response time at 18hr avg. Northwind campaign crew fully ramped, no issues. Tomorrow's batch of 8 apps is prepped and ready to go
8 new creators today. Response time 16hr. Petalcrest creator pool at 94% utilization. Had one QC flag but resolved same-day. Wednesday queue ready — 6 in final review
6 creators onboarded. 17hr response time. Harborline Foods campaign launched with full crew, 12 creators, zero delays. Caught a batch data error in pending apps, fixed same-day no impact
7 creators added. 19hr response time. Week total already at 28. Ridgeway Outdoors needed expedited onboarding for seasonal cohort — got 3 creators done in 12hr. Signature backlog cleared

5 creators today, 33 for the week (132% of target). Avg response time 17.8hr across the board. All campaigns staffed — Northwind, Petalcrest, Harborline, Ridgeway. Cinderhouse Coffee cohort queued for Monday. Zero escalations, clean week

Really great call with Northwind today, super positive energy on the renewal. They love the creative and we're almost locked on extending another year. Petalcrest had a good sync too, strong interest in expanding Q1 budget. Feeling good about both of these

Northwind still in a great place, another productive conversation. They're reviewing contracts internally, should have something by end of week. Petalcrest mentioned some budget concerns but I'm confident we can work through it. Overall momentum feels strong, really positive about the quarter

Northwind progressing, just in internal approvals which is normal. Expecting to hear back Friday. Petalcrest brought up pricing concerns but felt like a negotiation tactic, they're still interested. Had a few other positive calls with smaller accounts. Renewals tracking well

Northwind still in legal review, honestly a good sign that they're taking it seriously. They said it's almost there. Petalcrest feels like standard back and forth on terms. Had another encouraging call about their expansion plans. Really optimistic about how these are shaping up

Northwind still pending legal, they keep saying early next week. Frustrating but normal enterprise rhythm. Petalcrest called today and tbh the tone shifted — they're pausing expansion for "budget realignment" which is new. Still figuring out what changed. Pipeline is solid overall but keeping a close eye on these two, they're our biggest renewal plays

Cinderhouse ROAS 3.2x (target 2.8x), CPM down 8% WoW. Launched 2 experiments — Petalcrest carousel ads at 1.8% CTR early, Harborline video reels looking strong on engagement. CAC down 4% vs last week. One more experiment pending creative review

catching up on tue/wed — Petalcrest carousel hit 2.1% CTR, Harborline reels at 12% VTR. Launched third experiment wed, Ridgeway static ads audience test. CPM dropped another 3% on Cinderhouse. Portfolio ROAS 3.2x, CAC down 7% WTD. All three experiments performing

week close — 3 experiments total. Cinderhouse ROAS 3.2x, CPM down 11% WoW, CAC down 7%. Petalcrest carousel best at 2.4% CTR. Harborline reels stable 11% VTR. Ridgeway still in learning phase but early signals positive. Portfolio exceeded 2.8x target. Ready for scaling next week
45 dials today, 5 meetings booked. Northwind referral path converting really well. solid start to the week
48 dials, 4 meetings — Petalcrest, Harborline, Ridgeway, plus a net-new. locked in
46 dials, 3 meetings booked. had a reschedule but filled the slot quick. staying on the big accounts
44 dials, 2 meetings. some no-shows on callbacks but the ones who pick up are converting. still on pace
47 dials, 230+ for the week (crushed 200 target). strong outreach velocity all week. keeping momentum into next week

Matching v2 — still aligning with eng on algorithm requirements, speed vs accuracy tradeoff. They're reviewing infra constraints. Ran first user research session this morning with a creator cohort, really valuable feedback. Got another session tomorrow

Another sync with eng on the matching algorithm, they want more clarity on weighting before committing sprint capacity. Iterating on requirements, should have stakeholder input by Wednesday. Ran second research session with a brand client, good insights on creator filtering

Still aligning with eng on matching algo. They flagged our requirements might be overspecified so going back to prioritization with leadership. Scope shifting a bit. Had to reschedule this week's research session to next week, priorities shifted

Still in alignment phase on Matching v2 architecture. Eng has concerns about current proposal so doing another round of stakeholder input. Research sessions pushed to next week, had to prioritize internal alignment. Should have clearer direction by Friday

Spent the week on matching algorithm alignment, eng and product still iterating. Getting closer but slower than I'd like. Research is behind — only got 2 sessions done this week out of 5 target. Ramping up next week to catch up. Will get clarity on timeline next week
6 creators onboarded today. Response time at 21hr avg. Ridgeway Outdoors creator batch fully vetted. Tomorrow targeting 5 more apps from the queue.
5 creators onboarded today. Response time holding at 22hr. Petalcrest Beauty onboarding flow smooth with zero delays. Queue has 12 pending for tomorrow.
5 creators onboarded today. Response time ticking up to 22hr. Harborline Foods campaign roster locked and active. Prepping Thursday batch now.
6 creators onboarded today. Response time stable at 21hr. Cinderhouse Coffee creator cohort ramping up nicely. All systems go for Friday push.
5 creators onboarded today. Response time averaged 22hr for the week, slightly above baseline but under the 24hr target. Northwind Athletics final reviews done. Wrapping up at 27 total for the week.

Had a really fantastic conversation with the Northwind Athletics team this morning and the energy in the room felt incredibly aligned with where we want to take the partnership. We covered the renewal terms in depth and I can genuinely feel us moving past the final negotiation hurdles into a signed agreement phase, plus the Petalcrest Beauty stakeholders brought up some exciting expansion possibilities for next quarter that really validate our recent strategy shifts.

Northwind Athletics is officially signed on for the renewal and the whole team is buzzing about locking that in, which is such a massive win for our enterprise retention goals. On the Petalcrest front we actually dug into the expansion revenue numbers today and they are looking at committing an additional eighteen percent to their Q1 media budget, so I am just thrilled with how quickly this momentum is building across both accounts.

The Petalcrest Beauty expansion conversation continued and we finally mapped out the exact deliverable mix with them committing to a fifteen percent budget increase that directly hits our target for the quarter. Northwind is already fully operational on the new terms and the client success team over there is giving us incredibly warm feedback on the campaign rollout.

Spent the morning wrapping up final compliance docs for the Northwind renewal and everything is officially in the system which feels like such a huge weight lifted. Petalcrest expansion budget is fully approved by their procurement team and we are just waiting on the final green light from their VP to execute the additional spend.

Closing out the week on an incredibly high note with both major enterprise accounts moving exactly where we wanted them. Northwind is fully live and Petalcrest is ready to scale the new fifteen percent expansion package next week, leaving me feeling really confident about hitting our quarterly retention targets early.
CAC flat at 0 percent WoW change. CPM $14.20, CTR 1.4 percent. ROAS holding at 2.5x. Launched 1 experiment: Harborline video ads. Target CAC reduction remains unmet.
CAC unchanged. CPM $14.35, CTR 1.3 percent. ROAS steady 2.5x. Harborline engagement tracking normal. Pending 2 more experiments for weekly target.
CAC still 0 percent delta. CPM $14.50, CTR 1.2 percent. ROAS 2.4x. Second experiment launched: Cinderhouse carousel at 1.5 percent CTR. Weekly count 2 of 3. CAC not improving.
CAC flatline continues. CPM $14.40, CTR 1.35 percent. ROAS 2.5x. No new experiments today, reviewing Wednesday results. Weekly output capped at 2. CAC reduction stalled.
CAC unchanged for week. CPM $14.45, CTR 1.25 percent. ROAS 2.4x. Final tally: 2 experiments launched. CAC reduction target missed. Data exported for Monday optimization.
48 dials today, 6 meetings booked. Ridgeway Outdoors referral stream is looking pretty solid. Good momentum to start the week.
42 dials today, 8 meetings booked. Northwind Athletics contacts are actually picking up. Week is tracking right on pace.
45 dials today, 7 meetings booked. Petalcrest Beauty outreach hitting the mark. Keeping the pipeline full and steady.
50 dials today, 7 meetings booked. Harborline Foods leads are converting nicely. Almost at the weekly goal.
44 dials today, 6 meetings booked. Wrapped up the week at 229 total dials and 34 meetings booked overall. Solid week all around.

Spent the day iterating on the Creator Matching v2 spec and finally got the engineering team to align on the core algorithm parameters. The stakeholder input from design really helped clarify the speed versus accuracy tradeoff. We ran the first of our weekly research sessions today and gathered some really meaningful qualitative feedback on the onboarding friction points.

The engineering alignment from yesterday held up through the architecture review, which is a huge step forward. We blocked out time to run two more user research sessions to hit our weekly cadence. The product team is actively iterating on the feedback loops based on this morning's creator cohort.

Successfully completed the second and third research sessions today, bringing our weekly total to three. The v2 spec is now fully locked after incorporating the latest stakeholder input, and we are ready to move from planning into implementation. No major blockers remaining on the tech side.

Wrapping up the fourth user research session this afternoon, which puts us at four out of five for the week. The matching algorithm parameters are officially approved by the product lead and engineering, so we are transitioning straight into sprint planning for implementation. The qualitative data is painting a clear path forward.

Final research session shifted to next week but we closed out with four solid sessions this time around. The v2 spec is fully approved and locked, and engineering has already started pulling tickets for the first implementation phase. Feeling really good about the alignment and the tangible progress on the roadmap.
7 creators onboarded today. Response time holding steady at 22hr. Northwind and Petalcrest applicants all processed. Prepped 6 apps for Tuesday morning.

3 creators onboarded. Numbers down due to major platform outage mid-morning. Response time spiked to 25hr. Working through the backlog now. Goal for Wednesday is to clear the queue if systems stay up.
4 creators onboarded. Outage persisted through early afternoon creating a bottleneck. Response time currently at 28hr. Pending Ridgeway and Cinderhouse apps still in the queue for tomorrow.
8 creators onboarded as systems stabilized. Response time down to 26hr. Cleared half of the outage backlog. Harborline batch is fully ramped. Expecting strong finish Friday.
7 creators onboarded today. Response time recovered to 23hr. Weekly onboarding total reached 29 despite the mid-week outage. All systems normal for the weekend. Prepping Monday's queue.

Started the week with such a great sync with the Harborline Foods team and I am feeling so much positive energy about our partnership. They had a few final questions on the contract terms but the vibes are excellent. Also reached out to Ridgeway Outdoors to get our quarterly review on the calendar and I am looking forward to hearing back from them soon.

Incredible news today as the Harborline Foods renewal is officially signed and in the books which is such a huge win for the team. I had a quick chat with Ridgeway Outdoors earlier and although they seem a little swamped with their own internal projects I am sure we will get that expansion meeting locked in by the end of the week.

Still riding high on the Harborline news and spent most of the morning beginning the onboarding process for their expanded creator tier. Ridgeway has been a little quiet since yesterday but it is probably just a busy season for them and I am confident they will jump back into the thread once they clear their plates. Petalcrest also had some lovely things to say about our latest UGC batch.

Had a wonderful brainstorming session for the Cinderhouse Coffee account and they are showing so much interest in a Q4 expansion. I sent another follow up to the Ridgeway team just to keep us top of mind for them. Even though it has been a few days since we last connected I know they value the relationship and will reach out when the timing is right.

Wrapping up a really productive week with the Harborline renewal safely tucked away. I havent heard back from Ridgeway just yet but I am not worried at all since they are likely just heads down on their new product launch. I am planning to give them some space over the weekend and will check back in Monday with some fresh ideas for their expansion revenue goals.

catching up on mon/tue — launched experiment one mon which was northwind video creative split test. tue focused on cinderhouse audience scaling. launched experiment two today for petalcrest beauty influencers. portfolio roas 3.1x. cac down 4% wtd. cpm 14.50 across harborline.

launched experiment three today which was ridgeway outdoors retargeting logic. cac down 5% wtd. harborline reels ctr at 1.9%. cinderhouse search ads showing 3.4x roas. all experiments for the week now live and collecting data.

portfolio roas ended at 3.2x. cac maintained 5% reduction for the week. northwind experiment showing 12% lift in conversion. three experiments launched and stable. monday plan involves shifting budget to petalcrest top performers.
35 dials today and 2 meetings booked. northwind looks like a great lead. feeling good about the momentum.
32 dials and 1 meeting booked. bit of a slow day but had some really quality convos with harborline prospects.
30 dials today. 1 meeting booked with cinderhouse. short on the dial count but the people i did talk to were super engaged.
34 dials and 2 meetings on the books. petalcrest referral was a win. love the energy of these calls lately.
31 dials to wrap up the week. 1 meeting booked. total dials a bit lower than usual but the quality of the pipeline is looking sharp.

Matching v2 — focusing on internal alignment regarding the new data schema for creator profiles. Spent the afternoon in a deep dive with the engineering leads to understand how the new attributes will flow through. Completed the first user research session of the week with a group of beauty creators and the qualitative feedback was very illuminating.

Continuing to iterate on the matching algorithm components. There is some healthy debate among stakeholders about how much we should weight past performance versus recent engagement. Ran our second research session this morning which gave us more clarity on the creator dashboard experience. Still identifying potential blockers for the full release.

Excited to share that we officially shipped the core matching algorithm component to the staging environment today which is a solid step forward. However we are navigating a new blocker regarding the data pipeline integration that might impact our timeline for the full version. Completed a third research session today to keep the feedback loop tight.

Working through the data pipeline integration issue with the infra team to ensure we have total alignment on the fix. It is a complex process that requires more stakeholder input before we commit to a specific path. Iterating on the UI mocks based on the three research sessions we have completed so far this week.

Wrapping up the week with 3 total user research sessions completed which provided a wealth of process insights. The data pipeline blocker is still under review but we are making progress on the technical requirements. Matching v2 is moving along as we balance speed with the necessary architectural integrity.
8 creators onboarded today, cleared half of the backlog from last week's outage. Response time down to 17hr. Northwind campaign fully staffed. Tomorrow focusing on the remaining apps from the queue 🚀
9 creators onboarded, backlog is officially cleared. Response time at 15hr avg. Harborline Foods batch is complete. Smooth sailing rest of the week.
7 creators onboarded today. Response time holding steady at 15hr. Petalcrest Beauty expansion group is live. Maintaining volume with zero delay in support tickets.
8 creators onboarded. Response time 14hr avg. Ridgeway Outdoors campaign ready for launch. Already past the 25/week target with one day to go.
6 creators onboarded today, 38 total for the week. Final response time 15hr avg. Cinderhouse Coffee cohort is fully ramped. Best week in a while 📈

Had a really wonderful start to the week with the Cinderhouse Coffee team today and I am so thrilled to share that they officially signed their renewal. The energy on the call was just so positive and they are really excited to keep building with us for another year. Also had a lovely catch up with Harborline Foods where we started chatting about a potential 15% upsell on their Q2 buy which feels like a great direction for the partnership.

The good vibes are definitely continuing this week after another great session with the team at Northwind Athletics. I am so happy to report that the extension is signed and we are all set to keep the momentum going. It feels so good to have these major milestones locked in so early in the week and I am just really proud of how these relationships are blossoming lately.

Spent most of the morning in a really productive strategy session with Petalcrest Beauty and the creative direction they want to take is just so inspiring. We are seeing so much enthusiasm for the new creator matching features and it makes me so optimistic about our expansion goals. Harborline is also leaning further into that 15% increase for their next campaign so everything is feeling very aligned right now.

Really positive energy on the Ridgeway Outdoors check-in today as they were just glowing about the latest campaign results. We are having some very promising conversations about growing their presence on the platform and I am feeling very confident about where we are heading. It is just so rewarding to see these partners finding so much value in what we do ✨

Wrapping up such a strong week with a final sync with the Harborline Foods team and they have officially committed to that 15% expansion revenue for Q2. It has been such a joy to see so many signatures and smiles this week from Cinderhouse to Northwind. Feeling incredibly grateful for the team's support in getting these renewals across the finish line and I am heading into the weekend with a lot of excitement for what is next.
Portfolio ROAS 3.4x. CAC down 5% WoW. Launched experiment 1: Northwind video sequencing at 2.1% CTR. Cinderhouse display ads holding 2.9x ROAS. Reviewing Harborline creative for tomorrow's launch.

Launched experiment 2: Harborline influencer whitelist at 1.9% CTR early. CAC down 7% cumulative. Ridgeway search spend optimized, CPM down 12%. Overall ROAS 3.5x. Performance stable across all tiers.
Launched experiment 3: Petalcrest lookalike audience test. Initial ROAS 3.1x. Portfolio CAC down 9% vs last week. Northwind CTR steady at 2.2%. Monitoring frequency caps on Cinderhouse campaigns.
Launched experiment 4: Ridgeway multi-touch attribution test. Total experiments 4/3 target. CAC down 10%. Harborline ROAS increased to 3.6x. CPM avg across portfolio remains 10% below benchmark.

Weekly summary: CAC down 12%, ROAS 3.5x avg. 4 experiments live. Petalcrest test results show 15% lift in conversion. Cinderhouse scaling at 3.3x ROAS. All performance KPIs met or exceeded for the period 📊
42 dials today and 2 meetings booked. Northwind was a big win on the outreach front. feeling like the rhythm is coming back already.
hit 45 dials and landed 2 meetings. really solid conversations with some beauty brands that look promising. momentum is building.
48 dials today and 1 meeting on the calendar. the Harborline referral path is heating up but prospects are taking longer to commit ☕
another 46 dials and 1 meeting booked. lots of good convos but people want follow-ups next week. still grinding though.
finished with 45 dials for a total of 226 this week, crushed the 200 target. meetings came in at 7 which is a bit light but the pipeline quality feels strong heading into next week.

Matching v2 has officially moved into QA today which is a huge step for the team as we work toward cross-functional alignment on the final logic. Ran my first research session this afternoon with a high-volume creator and the qualitative insights regarding the UI were very enlightening. Iterating on the feedback loop for tomorrow's session.

Second user research session completed today with a focus on the new search filters and the stakeholder input is helping us refine the prioritization of the v2 rollout. Engineering reports that initial test results in QA look very good for the core matching algorithm. Continuing to monitor the speed vs accuracy balance as we move toward staging.

Third research session wrapped up this morning and we are seeing a lot of consistency in how creators want to interact with the marketplace. Matching v2 has been shipped to staging for internal dogfooding and the team is currently aligning on the final edge cases. Progress feels very steady and the outcomes are starting to materialize.

Just finished the fourth research session and the feedback from our enterprise partners on the new matching logic is very positive. The feature is holding up well in the staging environment and we are currently iterating on the final documentation for the release. Engaging with the design team to ensure stakeholder expectations are fully met before we move out of QA 🔍

Final research session of the week is done which brings us to a total of 5 sessions and a wealth of new insights to digest. Matching v2 is looking solid in the final QA gates and we are seeing very promising stability in the staging environment. It has been a week of real forward motion and I am very pleased with how the team has delivered on these milestones.
32 creators onboarded today, exceeding weekly pace. Response time avg at 21hr. Initial batch of Northwind applications processed. Focusing on queue volume for tomorrow.
29 creators onboarded. Response time ticked up to 23hr due to high volume. Cinderhouse campaign leads are current. Pushing to keep response metrics under the 24hr hard target.
30 creators onboarded. Response time holding at 22hr. Harborline intake complete. Maintaining the onboarding pace but monitoring the slight lag in creator comms.
27 creators onboarded. Response time at 23hr avg. Ridgeway Outdoors group live. Queue is heavy but manageable for the 25/wk goal.
31 creators onboarded today, total 149 for the week. Response time finished at 22hr. All weekend batches prepped for Petalcrest. Metrics stable but slow.

Really great energy coming off the weekend for Ridgeway Outdoors, they seem so excited about the platform and the momentum is palpable. Cinderhouse Coffee is also showing such positive signals in our latest threads. I am feeling very optimistic that we are almost there with both of these renewals. Just continuing to nurture these relationships and keep the good vibes flowing toward a close soon.

Had a wonderful touchpoint with the Ridgeway team and the synergy is just fantastic right now. They mentioned they are reviewing everything and it feels like we are on the verge of something big. Cinderhouse is also moving along with really great momentum and I can tell they appreciate our partnership. It is all about staying present and supportive as they finalize their internal thoughts.

The vibes with Cinderhouse Coffee are just so strong today after a quick check-in and they really seem to be leaning into the expansion idea. Ridgeway Outdoors is also showing incredible promise and I am just loving the direction of our conversations. Everything is trending in a beautiful direction and I am confident we will have some wonderful news to share very shortly.

Another day of fantastic momentum with both Ridgeway and Cinderhouse as we keep the dialogue open and positive. There is so much great alignment on the long-term vision and I can feel the excitement building on their side. Truly optimistic about where we stand and I believe we are just moments away from locking these in. Just keeping the energy high and the support consistent.

Ending the week on such a high note with these accounts because the sentiment is just so warm and encouraging. Ridgeway Outdoors and Cinderhouse Coffee both feel like they are right at the finish line and the momentum is honestly better than ever. I am so happy with how these relationships are maturing and I am looking forward to a very successful outcome soon.
Petalcrest ROAS 2.1x (target 2.2x). CPM flat WoW. Launched 1 experiment — Harborline static image vs video. CAC $42 (no change). CTR 0.9% on top funnel.
Northwind ROAS 2.4x. CPM up 2% on Meta. CAC remains flat at $42. Harborline experiment results inconclusive. Monitoring spend efficiency across active channels.
Cinderhouse ROAS 2.0x. Launched 2nd experiment of the week — Ridgeway Outdoors lookalike audiences. CAC $43. CTR 1.1% on seasonal creative. Under target on experiment volume.
Weekly CAC flat. Total experiments 2 (target 3). Petalcrest carousel underperforming at 1.5x ROAS. Ridgeway experiment needs more data. Maintaining current bids.
55 dials today including all my follow-up attempts and redials. 3 meetings booked. solid effort on the Ridgeway outbound list.
50 dials logged after clearing the callback queue for the morning. 3 meetings on the board. Northwind leads are okay but could be better.
52 dials if you count the second attempts on the Cinderhouse list. 3 meetings booked. keeping the volume high and staying busy.
54 dials today with a lot of redials in the mix to keep the numbers up. 3 meetings booked. Harborline outreach is a grind but we are pushing through.
51 dials to finish the week including some quick follow-up pings. 3 meetings booked. glad to hit the dial volume even if the bookings are steady.

Moving back into an alignment phase for Creator Matching v2 after QA flagged several critical bugs in the build. Need to facilitate a session with engineering to understand the scope of the fixes. User research is on hold while we iterate on the technical architecture.

Still working through concerns from the engineering team regarding the recent bug reports. Deep in stakeholder input sessions to ensure the next iteration addresses the stability issues. It feels like we are finding common ground but the process takes time.

Managed to run 1 user research session today but the focus remains primarily on internal alignment. Iterating on the proposed fix for the v2 architecture based on developer feedback. We are committed to getting the process right before moving forward.

Continuing the dialogue with stakeholders to clear the blockers identified by QA. The team is still in the alignment phase but the discussions are helping to surface hidden complexities. Should have more clarity on the roadmap soon once we agree on the rework.

Ending the week in an iteration cycle as we refine the Matching v2 logic. Spent most of the day in alignment meetings to finalize the plan for the critical bugs. Research was light this week with only 1 session completed but the internal process felt necessary.
32 new creators onboarded. Response time 15hr. Northwind and Petalcrest pools at 100% utilization. Zero QC flags. Tuesday queue set with 30 in final review
34 new creators onboarded. Response time 14hr. Harborline and Ridgeway fully staffed. One minor QC flag resolved same-day. Wednesday queue ready with 33 pending
35 new creators onboarded. Response time 15hr. Cinderhouse and Petalcrest at full coverage. Clean pipeline, no escalations. Thursday queue at 31 in review
31 new creators onboarded. Response time 16hr. Northwind and Ridgeway fully staffed. Zero backlog. Friday queue holding 29 final approvals
33 new creators onboarded. Response time 14hr. All brand pools fully staffed across board. No QC issues. Best week throughput, next week queue prepped at 28

Had a really constructive start to the week with Harborline and Petalcrest, both conversations felt productive even though there are still some internal approvals on their side. Harborline mentioned some concerns but overall energy felt positive and collaborative, so feeling like we’re still in a good place as we work through details

Petalcrest follow-up today and they introduced a budget freeze conversation which wasn’t on the radar before, still trying to understand scope but keeping things moving and staying optimistic. Harborline continuing discussions, some hesitation but still engaged which feels like a good sign overall

Petalcrest expansion officially paused due to budget freeze, which is definitely a shift but trying to reframe around long-term relationship and future opportunity. Harborline call felt a bit more uncertain than earlier in the week, still hoping to stabilize things but starting to feel less predictable

Harborline decided not to renew which was unexpected and honestly a tough call to process given how conversations started earlier. Petalcrest still frozen on expansion. Trying to redirect focus toward other accounts in pipeline but definitely a heavier day overall

Still processing the Harborline loss and Petalcrest freeze, both significant hits this week and not what we planned for. Conversations with other accounts are ongoing but this was a tough reset moment. Focusing on rebuilding momentum going into next week
start week — 2 experiments launched. Northwind ROAS 3.1x, CPM flat WoW, CAC down 5%. Petalcrest CTR 2.2%. Harborline video VTR 10%. pacing stable
week close — 2 experiments total. Cinderhouse ROAS 3.0x, CPM down 8%, CAC down 6%. Ridgeway CTR 2.5%. portfolio avg 2.9x. stable performance
47 dials, 2 meetings — Northwind and Ridgeway, solid start
49 dials, 2 meetings — Petalcrest and Cinderhouse, feeling good
45 dials, 1 meeting — Harborline, quick win
50 dials, 2 meetings — Ridgeway and net-new, stacking nicely
48 dials, 3 meetings — Northwind, Petalcrest, Cinderhouse, crushed it this week

Spent time finalizing Creator Matching v2 requirements with engineering and aligning on final edge cases before release. Completed 1 research session with a brand client which helped validate filtering assumptions, feeling close to pushing something live

Wrapped final prep for v2 beta and confirmed deployment plan with engineering. Ran another research session bringing total to 2 so far, insights are consistent and reinforcing prioritization decisions. No major blockers heading into launch

Creator Matching v2 beta pushed to production today and is now live for initial users. Deployed successfully with no critical issues. Completed third research session post-launch to gather immediate feedback which is already proving useful

Monitoring v2 beta performance and iterating on minor bugs identified by early users, nothing blocking but a few edge cases around filtering logic. Completed fourth research session, feedback largely positive with some actionable tweaks

Continued iteration on v2 beta with a couple of small bug fixes deployed, system is stable and adoption trending in the right direction. No new research sessions today so total at 4, focusing on consolidating insights and planning next iteration
